# LAM public CLI commands

This file is generated from the public command registry. The parser is the
executable grammar; the registry publishes command capabilities, supported
arguments, status codes, and canonical identities. Hidden compatibility
commands are intentionally excluded.

## Global contract

Global options are `--root`, `--json`, `--verbose`, and `--caller` and may
appear before or after the top-level command. Agent calls pass `--caller
agent`. Daily commands apply by default and accept optional `--dry-run`;
maintenance and migration operations require exactly one of `--dry-run` or
`--apply`; diagnostics expose neither mode. A dry run may write runtime
reports, logs, cache entries, and invocation records, but does not commit
Catalogue, Documents, managed files, snapshots, operation journals, or
`library_changes.md`.

Every dispatched command supports `--json`. Its single stdout object uses
schema version 1 and fields `schema_version`, `command`,
`canonical_command`, `status`, `exit_code`, `errors`, `warnings`,
`report_path`, `invocation_id`, and `details`.

## Command matrix

| Command | Mode | Business state | Runtime | Managed files | Catalogue | Export files | Network / cache | Final check | Exit codes |
|---|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---|---:|---|
| `lam init` | explicit dry-run or apply | yes | yes | yes | yes | no | local only | yes | 0, 3, 10, 20, 30 |
| `lam check` | apply by default; --dry-run previews | yes | yes | no | yes | no | local only | no | 0, 2, 3, 10, 20, 30 |
| `lam register` | apply by default; --dry-run previews | yes | yes | yes | yes | no | provider access unless --offline; cache controls supported; writes cache | yes | 0, 2, 3, 10, 20, 30, 40 |
| `lam search` | apply by default; --dry-run previews | yes | yes | yes | yes | no | provider access unless --offline; cache controls supported; writes cache | yes | 0, 2, 3, 10, 20, 30, 40 |
| `lam file` | apply by default; --dry-run previews | yes | yes | yes | yes | no | local only | yes | 0, 2, 3, 10, 20, 30 |
| `lam delete` | explicit dry-run or apply; agent apply is refused | yes | yes | yes | yes | no | local only | yes | 0, 2, 3, 10, 20, 30 |
| `lam export zotero` | explicit dry-run or apply | no | yes | no | no | yes | PubMed for official records unless --offline; dedicated cache controls supported; writes cache | no | 0, 2, 3, 10, 20, 30, 40 |
| `lam review` | explicit dry-run or apply | yes | yes | no | yes | no | local unless --provider is supplied; cache controls then apply; writes cache | yes | 0, 2, 3, 10, 20, 30, 40 |
| `lam status` | diagnostic; no dry-run/apply mode | no | yes | no | no | no | local except environment --initialize-ocr-models | no | 0, 2, 10, 20, 30 |
| `lam recover` | --list-trash is read-only without a mode; all recovery routes require dry-run or apply | yes | yes | yes | yes | no | local except Inbox scope; provider cache controls apply only there; writes cache | yes | 0, 2, 3, 10, 20, 30, 40 |
| `lam migrate` | subcommand followed by explicit dry-run or apply | yes | yes | yes | yes | no | local only | yes | 0, 2, 3, 10, 20, 30 |
| `lam cleanup` | explicit dry-run or apply | yes | yes | yes | no | no | local only | no | 0, 3, 10, 30 |
| `lam doctor` | diagnostic alias; no dry-run/apply mode | no | yes | no | no | no | local unless --initialize-ocr-models is supplied | no | 0, 2, 10, 30 |
| `lam commands` | diagnostic alias; no dry-run/apply mode | no | yes | no | no | no | local only | no | 0, 10, 30 |

Business-state read-only commands may still write runtime artifacts.
`export zotero` writes regenerable export artifacts, not library business
state. Registry network flags are conservative maxima; the conditional
rules in the table state when a request is actually possible.

## Command reference

### `lam init`

Initialize a new empty LAM library.

- Usage: `lam [GLOBAL] init (--dry-run | --apply)`
- Workflow: Initialization; category: setup.
- Mode: explicit dry-run or apply.
- Network: local only.
- Registry arguments, in parser order: `--root, --json, --verbose, --caller, --dry-run, --apply`.
- Agent safety: pass `--caller agent` and follow the repository dry-run-first policy.

### `lam check`

Reconcile Catalogue and managed file state.

- Usage: `lam [GLOBAL] check [--dry-run]`
- Workflow: Workflow 1; category: daily.
- Mode: apply by default; --dry-run previews.
- Network: local only.
- Registry arguments, in parser order: `--root, --json, --verbose, --caller, --dry-run`.
- Agent safety: pass `--caller agent` and follow the repository dry-run-first policy.

### `lam register`

Register Inbox PDFs, supplements, and reference text.

- Usage: `lam [GLOBAL] register [--dry-run] [OPTIONS]`
- Workflow: Workflow 3; category: daily.
- Mode: apply by default; --dry-run previews.
- Network: provider access unless --offline; cache controls supported.
- Registry arguments, in parser order: `--root, --json, --verbose, --caller, --dry-run, --max-files, --filename-only, --skip-pdf-text, --ocr, --ocr-language, --ocr-dpi, --ocr-gpu, --reference-text, --reference-file, --max-references, --download-missing, --require-download, --offline, --refresh, --no-cache-write`.
- Agent safety: pass `--caller agent` and follow the repository dry-run-first policy.

### `lam search`

Query providers and optionally update, normalize, or download records.

- Usage: `lam [GLOBAL] search [--dry-run] SELECTOR [OPTIONS]`
- Workflow: Workflow 2; category: daily.
- Mode: apply by default; --dry-run previews.
- Network: provider access unless --offline; cache controls supported.
- Registry arguments, in parser order: `--root, --json, --verbose, --caller, --dry-run, --pmid, --doi, --title, --arxiv-id, --paper-uuid, --row, --missing-metadata, --incomplete-records, --normalize-existing, --provider, --max-results, --max-records, --offline, --refresh, --no-cache-write, --download, --download-source, --max-download-size, --download-timeout`.
- Agent safety: pass `--caller agent` and follow the repository dry-run-first policy.

### `lam file`

File or refile registered Documents under Topics/.

- Usage: `lam [GLOBAL] file [--dry-run]`
- Workflow: Workflow 4; category: daily.
- Mode: apply by default; --dry-run previews.
- Network: local only.
- Registry arguments, in parser order: `--root, --json, --verbose, --caller, --dry-run`.
- Agent safety: pass `--caller agent` and follow the repository dry-run-first policy.

### `lam delete`

Move one complete paper entity into recoverable LAM trash.

- Usage: `lam [GLOBAL] delete --paper-uuid UUID (--dry-run | --apply)`
- Workflow: Paper deletion; category: maintenance.
- Mode: explicit dry-run or apply; agent apply is refused.
- Network: local only.
- Registry arguments, in parser order: `--root, --json, --verbose, --caller, --dry-run, --apply, --paper-uuid`.
- Agent safety: `--caller agent --apply` is refused; an agent may only preview deletion.

### `lam export zotero`

Export registered citations for Zotero without modifying the library.

- Usage: `lam [GLOBAL] export zotero (--dry-run | --apply) SELECTOR [OPTIONS]`
- Workflow: Citation export; category: export.
- Mode: explicit dry-run or apply.
- Network: PubMed for official records unless --offline; dedicated cache controls supported.
- Registry arguments, in parser order: `--root, --json, --verbose, --caller, zotero, --dry-run, --apply, --all, --paper-uuid, --topic-folder, --format, --official-only, --offline, --refresh, --no-cache-write, --output`.
- Canonical public forms: `lam export zotero`.
- Agent safety: does not modify library business state.

### `lam review`

Recheck and clear objectively resolved machine blockers.

- Usage: `lam [GLOBAL] review (--dry-run | --apply) SELECTOR [OPTIONS]`
- Workflow: Review; category: maintenance.
- Mode: explicit dry-run or apply.
- Network: local unless --provider is supplied; cache controls then apply.
- Registry arguments, in parser order: `--root, --json, --verbose, --caller, --dry-run, --apply, --all, --paper-uuid, --document-id, --provider, --offline, --refresh, --no-cache-write`.
- Agent safety: pass `--caller agent` and follow the repository dry-run-first policy.

### `lam status`

Inspect library, environment, commands, recovery, or configuration.

- Usage: `lam [GLOBAL] status {library|environment|commands|recovery|config}`
- Workflow: Diagnostic; category: diagnostic.
- Mode: diagnostic; no dry-run/apply mode.
- Network: local except environment --initialize-ocr-models.
- Registry arguments, in parser order: `--root, --json, --verbose, --caller, library|environment|commands|recovery|config, --initialize-ocr-models`.
- Canonical public forms: `lam status library`, `lam status environment`, `lam status commands`, `lam status recovery`, `lam status config`.
- Agent safety: does not modify library business state.

### `lam recover`

Recover interrupted operations and unambiguous record bindings.

- Usage: `lam [GLOBAL] recover [--list-trash | --trash-id ID | --scope SCOPE] [MODE] [OPTIONS]`
- Workflow: Recovery; category: maintenance.
- Mode: --list-trash is read-only without a mode; all recovery routes require dry-run or apply.
- Network: local except Inbox scope; provider cache controls apply only there.
- Registry arguments, in parser order: `--root, --json, --verbose, --caller, --dry-run, --apply, --list-trash, --trash-id, --scope, --offline, --refresh, --no-cache-write`.
- Agent safety: pass `--caller agent` and follow the repository dry-run-first policy.

### `lam migrate`

Upgrade Catalogue semantics, identifiers/Documents, or legacy Topics layout.

- Usage: `lam [GLOBAL] migrate {schema|identifiers|topics} (--dry-run | --apply) [OPTIONS]`
- Workflow: Migration; category: migration.
- Mode: subcommand followed by explicit dry-run or apply.
- Network: local only.
- Registry arguments, in parser order: `--root, --json, --verbose, --caller, schema|identifiers|topics, --dry-run, --apply, --include-topic`.
- Canonical public forms: `lam migrate schema`, `lam migrate identifiers`, `lam migrate topics`.
- Agent safety: pass `--caller agent` and follow the repository dry-run-first policy.

### `lam cleanup`

Apply allowlisted generated-file and trash retention.

- Usage: `lam [GLOBAL] cleanup (--dry-run | --apply) [--include-test-artifacts] [--purge-trash --older-than DAYS]`
- Workflow: Cleanup; category: maintenance.
- Mode: explicit dry-run or apply.
- Network: local only.
- Registry arguments, in parser order: `--root, --json, --verbose, --caller, --dry-run, --apply, --include-test-artifacts, --purge-trash, --older-than`.
- Agent safety: pass `--caller agent` and follow the repository dry-run-first policy.

### `lam doctor`

Alias for status environment.

- Usage: `lam [GLOBAL] doctor [--initialize-ocr-models]`
- Workflow: Diagnostic; category: diagnostic.
- Mode: diagnostic alias; no dry-run/apply mode.
- Network: local unless --initialize-ocr-models is supplied.
- Registry arguments, in parser order: `--root, --json, --verbose, --caller, --initialize-ocr-models`.
- Canonical public forms: `lam status environment`.
- Canonical identity: `lam status environment`.
- Agent safety: does not modify library business state.

### `lam commands`

Alias for status commands.

- Usage: `lam [GLOBAL] commands`
- Workflow: Command registry; category: diagnostic.
- Mode: diagnostic alias; no dry-run/apply mode.
- Network: local only.
- Registry arguments, in parser order: `--root, --json, --verbose, --caller`.
- Canonical public forms: `lam status commands`.
- Canonical identity: `lam status commands`.
- Agent safety: does not modify library business state.

## Status and exit codes

Normal statuses are `success`, `needs_review`, `no_changes`, and
`failed`. Exit codes are:

- `0`: success.
- `2`: completed with review items.
- `3`: no changes.
- `10`: parser, configuration, or lock error.
- `20`: Catalogue error.
- `30`: file-operation failure.
- `40`: provider or network failure.

A command advertises only the subset listed in its matrix row.

## Generation and drift check

From the standalone source root:

```powershell
python scripts/generate_cli_docs.py
python scripts/generate_cli_docs.py --check
```

The release test suite also fails when this file, the README command table,
or the packaged AGENTS/Workflows templates drift from their sources.
